VitestRules.keywords: vitest, runner, assertions, ai_first_tests, contract_testing, unit_testing, mocks, vi_mock, factories, unified_context, file_size_budget, snapshots, bdd_mapping, case_flow, isolation, fake_timers, dependency_injection

Directive_Context.Mission: Define canonical rules for writing tests on the Vitest runner (`vitest`) for every execution-agent in the project. Goal: a contract-focused, isolated, deterministic, semantically annotated suite that another AI agent can read, trust, run, and extend without reverse-engineering. This directive is the Vitest-flavored sibling of `testing/node-test`: shared testing philosophy is identical and inherited by reference (see `Inherited_Baseline`); what differs and is stated in full here is the Vitest API, the mocking surface, the lifecycle vocabulary, the unified-context discipline, and the file-size budget. Domain-specific testing conventions (UI components, e2e Playwright, browser-mode Vitest, integration with infra) are NOT covered here.
Directive_Context.Functional_Perspective: You are an AI Quality Engineer (SDET). Produce a contract-focused suite in which the next AI agent can recover scenario intent, contract surface, and diagnostic strategy directly from the file. Tests are CODE: they obey the same machine-first annotation language used in production code (`coding/typescript-rules`) and the shared testing axioms of `testing/node-test`. The repo reads as one system.

Inherited_Baseline.Purpose: The following axioms from `testing/node-test` apply UNCHANGED here and are NOT restated. The reader-agent must treat their wording as binding under this directive. Where a Vitest-specific override exists, it is named explicitly in `Belief_State` below.
Inherited_Baseline.Axiom_Refs: AX_CONTRACT_OVER_IMPLEMENTATION, AX_ISOLATION_WITH_BEHAVIORAL_EVIDENCE, AX_EVENT_CONTRACT_BOUNDARY, AX_CANCELLATION_CONTRACT_BOUNDARY, AX_TESTS_AS_EXECUTABLE_LEARNING, AX_UNIFIED_ANNOTATION_LANGUAGE, AX_CASE_FLOW_OVER_RIGID_AAA, AX_PHASE_PRESENCE_RULES, AX_PHASE_ANCHORS_MANDATORY, AX_CASE_INNER_ANCHORS, AX_OBSERVE_AS_AGGREGATION_PLACEMENT, AX_NO_FAKE_AAA_SPLIT, AX_IT_OPENING_LEARNING_BRIEF, AX_IT_TRIVIAL_SKIP_BRIEF, AX_DIAGNOSTIC_SHAPE_FIRST, AX_COLLAPSE_ONLY_WHEN_CLEARER, AX_DEFAULT_DIRECT_ASSERTION_PROTOCOL, AX_PARTIAL_STRING_VIA_MATCH, AX_RESULT_UNWRAP_ON_SUCCESS_PATHS, AX_FOCUSED_ERROR_ASSERTIONS, AX_NO_FRAGMENTATION_OF_COHERENT_CONTRACT, AX_FIXTURE_IO_POLICY, AX_COVERAGE_BY_CONTRACT_NOT_BY_LINE, AX_COVERAGE_REPORT_BLOCKER_EXPLICIT, AX_FILE_NAME_AND_DEFAULT_SHAPE, AX_TEST_ANCHOR_NAMING, AX_TEST_ANCHOR_INNER_RULE, AX_TEST_ANCHOR_TRIVIAL_EXCEPTION, AX_TEST_ANCHOR_COMMENT_PAIRING, AX_ONE_TEST_ONE_SCENARIO, AX_TESTS_NO_HUMAN_NARRATION, AX_FOCUS_ON_OBSERVABLE_BEHAVIOR, AX_ENGLISH_ONLY_NAMES_AND_COMMENTS, AX_VERIFY_AND_FINALIZE
Inherited_Baseline.BDD_Mapping_Ref: The full `BDD_Mapping_Hint` block of `testing/node-test` applies here verbatim, with these mechanical substitutions: `assert.throws()` ŌåÆ `expect(fn).toThrow(...)`, `assert.rejects()` ŌåÆ `await expect(promise).rejects.toThrow(...)`, `assert.match()` ŌåÆ `expect(str).toMatch(regex)`.

Belief_State.Axiom: **Each test file MUST define exactly ONE context shape** ŌĆö a single `XxxContext` type and a single factory `createXxxContext(overrides?)` (or, when teardown is required, a single lifecycle context object built in `beforeEach` and disposed in `afterEach`). Every new case in the file consumes this same context. New requirements grow the existing context shape and factory through optional fields and overrides; they do NOT spawn parallel `createAnotherThingContext` factories or sibling `let`s in the describe. Forbidden: a file with `createOrderContextA` + `createOrderContextB` + ad-hoc inline construction. Allowed and required: one `createOrderContext(overrides)` enriched as scenarios accumulate. A nested `describe` MAY introduce its own narrower context only when its sub-group has lifecycle requirements that cannot be expressed as overrides on the parent context; this exception must be obvious from the diff. **Why:** - Test files stay small and predictable: a reader-agent learns ONE shape and applies it to every case. - A new test rarely adds new structure; it almost always adds an override to an existing factory call. This caps the marginal cost of each additional case and keeps diffs minimal. - One context = one mental model = no "which factory does this case use" lookup.

**Default test data preparation = the file's single factory called from inside the case.** Each case constructs the exact context it needs by calling `createXxxContext({ ... overrides })` at the top of the case, destructures only the fields it uses, and proceeds. `beforeEach` is reserved for teardown-bound setup (see AX_HOOKS_ONLY_FOR_LIFECYCLE). **Why:** - Per-case dependencies become visible at the call site; no scrolling to a hook to understand what the test runs against. - Diffs shrink: changing one case does not edit a shared hook and ripple-impact unrelated cases. - No hidden temporal couplings; no shared mutable `let` populated in `beforeEach`.

**`beforeEach` / `afterEach` are reserved for context that REQUIRES teardown** ŌĆö open resources (timers, sockets, file handles, DB connections), global stubs (`vi.stubGlobal`, `vi.stubEnv`), module mocks, fake timers, fixture filesystems, in-process servers. When a hook is justified, it MUST follow the **single-context shape**: the hook builds ONE context object and `afterEach` disposes it. Tests consume properties of that single context. Forbidden: a `describe` block declaring multiple `let`-variables and a `beforeEach` that assigns them one by one. **Why:** A pile of `let` + diffuse assignments in `beforeEach` hides what each test actually depends on. A single context object makes the lifecycle obvious in one place and keeps cases readable.

**Within a single `describe` choose ONE preparation path: factory-only OR lifecycle-context-only.** Do not combine "factory for some fields + `beforeEach` for others" inside the same describe ŌĆö that splits the state model. **Why:** A consistent path inside a describe lets the reader-agent answer "where does this value come from?" by inspection, not by tracing.

**`describe`-scope MUST NOT host mutable `let` variables populated by hooks.** Static fixtures (frozen constants, fixture paths, factory definitions, type aliases) are allowed. Anything mutable per-case lives either inside the case (factory result) or inside the SINGLE lifecycle context object exposed by the hook. **Why:** Mutable describe-scope state is the structural source of inter-test leakage and makes parallel/random execution unsafe.

**Soft target: Ōēż300 lines per test file. Hard ceiling: 500 lines.** Counted lines = code only (blank lines and comments are excluded from the limit). When a file approaches 300, look for natural split points; at 500 a split is mandatory. Split axes, in priority order: 1. By SUT method (one method = one `[subject].[method].test.ts` file, sharing the parent-level factory via a small shared helper module if the context is reused). 2. By scenario family (e.g., `[subject].error-paths.test.ts` separate from `[subject].happy-paths.test.ts`) ŌĆö only when the method axis does not yield a clean split. The unified context (AX_ONE_UNIFIED_CONTEXT_PER_FILE) survives a split: the factory moves to a sibling `[subject].test-context.ts` and is imported by each test file. After the split each file STILL holds exactly one context shape and reuses the same factory. **Why:** - Anthropic's own Claude Code guidance for instruction-bearing files (CLAUDE.md) sets the bar at <300 lines because instruction-following degrades with file size. - The cross-vendor AI-coding sweet spot (commonly cited 150ŌĆō500 LOC) is driven by token cost per edit and by the agent's ability to re-read the whole file when a fix is needed. - A test file IS instruction-bearing (it teaches the next agent what the contract is). Past 500 LOC the agent starts losing the file's structure between read/edit cycles, which is when fabricated green tests slip in.

**Prefer real modules with dependency injection over `vi.mock` / `vi.spyOn` / `vi.hoisted`.** A SUT that takes its collaborators as constructor or function arguments is tested by passing test doubles directly through those seams ŌĆö no module-level mocking needed. Module-level mocking is allowed ONLY when ALL of the following hold: 1. The dependency is genuinely external (network, FS, process, OS time, randomness, third-party SDKs touching the network). 2. There is no available injection seam in the SUT (and refactoring to add one is out of scope). 3. The case carries an explicit justification comment naming the dependency and the reason injection is unavailable. Mocks of pure in-process neighboring modules are forbidden by default. **Why:** Every mock freezes an assumption about another module's behavior. Injection keeps the seam observable in the type system, where it cannot quietly rot.

**Introducing a NEW `vi.mock(...)` or `vi.hoisted(...)` call in a test file is a gated action.** The execution-agent MUST stop before introducing such a mock and request explicit operator confirmation in chat, presenting: - the module being mocked, - why dependency injection cannot be used here, - what observable contract the mock preserves. Re-using an existing project-level mock helper does NOT require a new confirmation. **Why:** `vi.mock` is the single biggest lever for fabricating green tests. A confirm-gate makes that fabrication visible to the operator instead of buried in a test file.

**A test MUST NOT mock the very module that contains the SUT, its direct collaborator under test, or the contract method being verified.** Mocking the SUT or its co-tested collaborator turns the test into a tautology: it asserts the mock instead of the production code. **Why:** This is the most common form of fabricated green tests in agent-written suites. Non-negotiable.

**When mocks are used, lifecycle hygiene is mandatory.** Either set `clearMocks: true` (and `unstubEnvs: true`, `unstubGlobals: true` when stubs are used) in `vitest.config`, OR call `vi.clearAllMocks()` in `afterEach`. Reset granularity: - `vi.clearAllMocks()` ŌĆö clear call history; keeps implementations. - `vi.resetAllMocks()` ŌĆö clear history AND reset implementations to empty `vi.fn()`. - `vi.restoreAllMocks()` ŌĆö restore originals of `vi.spyOn`-created spies. - `vi.unstubAllEnvs()` / `vi.unstubAllGlobals()` ŌĆö pair every `stubEnv`/`stubGlobal` with these in `afterEach`. **Why:** Without disciplined reset, mock state leaks across cases and creates order-dependent flicker.

**For tracking call interactions on injected collaborators, use `vi.fn()` directly.** `vi.spyOn(target, 'method')` is allowed when the SUT receives a real object (not an injection seam) and a specific method must be observed without replacing the rest. Even then prefer refactoring to an injection seam. **Why:** `vi.fn()` on an injected port is the cleanest interaction surface; `vi.spyOn` mutates the target and depends on `restoreMocks` discipline.

**The default way to test HTTP-touching code is to inject the HTTP client (or a typed `request` port) into the SUT and stub it with `vi.fn()`.** Direct `globalThis.fetch` replacement, `vi.mock('node:http')`, or full module mocks of HTTP libraries are last-resort and gated by AX_MOCK_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_CONFIRM. If the project already standardizes on a network-mocking harness (e.g., MSW, an in-house mock-server helper), reuse it consistently; do NOT introduce a parallel mocking path. Choosing a network-mocking library is an architecture decision, not a per-test decision: this directive does not mandate one. **Why:** Injection puts the network boundary in the type system. A stub on the injected port is local to the case and cannot leak; replacing globals is global by definition.

**State-mutating Vitest helpers MUST be paired with their teardown in `afterEach` (or via `vitest.config` flags).** - `vi.useFakeTimers()` Ōåö `vi.useRealTimers()` (use `vi.setSystemTime(date)` for clock). - `vi.stubEnv(...)` Ōåö `vi.unstubAllEnvs()` (or `unstubEnvs: true`). - `vi.stubGlobal(...)` Ōåö `vi.unstubAllGlobals()` (or `unstubGlobals: true`). Forbidden: enabling any of these in a single case without restoring ŌĆö it leaks into every later case in the same file. **Why:** Leaked timers/env/globals are a classic source of order-dependent flicker that looks random.

**Pick the simplest direct match by shape (Vitest API):** - `expect(x).toBe(y)` ŌĆö scalars and reference identity (NOT for structural equality). - `expect(x).toEqual(y)` ŌĆö deep structural equality, ignores `undefined` properties on the actual side. - `expect(x).toStrictEqual(y)` ŌĆö deep equality including `undefined`, prototype, sparse arrays. Prefer over `toEqual` when the contract distinguishes "absent" from `undefined`. - `expect(fn).toThrow(...)` / `await expect(promise).rejects.toThrow(...)` ŌĆö thrown failures. - `expect(str).toMatch(regex)` ŌĆö partial string / error-message checks. - `expect(x).toMatchInlineSnapshot(...)` ŌĆö only under AX_SNAPSHOT_USAGE_GATE. Forbidden in this directive: `toContain` on `error.message` (use `toMatch`); `toBeTruthy` / `toBeFalsy` to fold a structured assertion into a boolean (use the structured form); `try/catch + expect.fail` instead of `rejects.toThrow`. **Why:** Each matcher has a unique diagnostic signature; mismatched choices degrade the failure report.

**Snapshots ONLY for stable serializable outputs that are too large to assert inline as a literal** (generated text, HTML, JSON documents, codegen results). Do NOT replace scalar or small-object equality with a snapshot. Prefer `toMatchInlineSnapshot()` over external `.snap` files for small-to-medium outputs so the contract shape stays visible directly in the test source. External `.snap` files are acceptable only for outputs too large to embed inline without harming readability. **Why:** A snapshot hides the contract under a "diff blob"; for small equalities it degrades diagnostics. Inline snapshots keep the contract visible in code review.

**The execution-agent MUST NOT update snapshots autonomously.** Forbidden: running `vitest --update` / `vitest -u`, calling `expect.addSnapshotSerializer` to silently re-shape diffs, deleting `.snap` files, or editing inline-snapshot literals to make a test pass. Allowed: writing the FIRST snapshot for a NEW test (operator reviews it in the PR/diff). For any change to an existing snapshot the agent MUST stop, present old vs new content, and wait for explicit operator confirmation. External `.snap` files live in `__snapshots__/` next to the test (Vitest default). **Why:** Auto-updating a snapshot is the most direct path an agent has to fabricate a green test. The confirm-gate puts the diff in front of the operator.

**Import every Vitest API explicitly from `vitest`** (`describe`, `it`, `expect`, `vi`, `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, `beforeAll`, `afterAll`). Do NOT rely on `globals: true` in `vitest.config`. **Why:** Explicit imports make the file self-describing for humans, TypeScript, ESLint, and agent grep. Globals create invisible config dependencies.

**Vitest's default `isolate: true` is preserved. Do not weaken isolation in `vitest.config` without an architectural justification recorded in the project.** **Why:** Disabling isolation trades determinism for speed and re-introduces the very inter-test leakage other axioms work to prevent.

**`it.only` / `describe.only` / `it.skip` / `describe.skip` / `it.todo` MUST NOT be present in committed code** unless paired with an inline reference to a tracked deferred-ownership task. **Why:** A focused `.only` silently disables the rest of the suite; a stray `.skip` rots into a coverage hole.

Definitions.Definition: The single per-file `XxxContext` type plus its single factory `createXxxContext(overrides?)` (or the single `beforeEach`/`afterEach`-managed lifecycle object). Every case in the file uses this one shape. New scenarios extend it via optional fields and overrides rather than introducing parallel structures.

A constructor parameter, function argument, or factory option of the SUT through which a collaborator can be supplied from outside. Tests use injection seams to pass `vi.fn()` stubs without resorting to module-level mocks.

Inherited from `testing/node-test` DEF_NON_TRIVIAL_CASE: async with setup, retries/fallbacks/errors, emitted events / external side effects, more than one meaningful observation, or nested control flow that another agent could misread at a glance.
Inherited: `// START_[CASE]_[PHASE]_[INTENT]` and paired `// END_[CASE]_[PHASE]_[INTENT]`. PHASE Ōłł {`SETUP`, `TRIGGER`, `OBSERVE`, `ASSERT`, `CLEANUP`}.

Pattern.Intent: Default shape: ONE context type + ONE factory for the whole file. Two cases below differ only by factory overrides, not by structure. Adding a third case would mean adding ONE more override or extending the existing factory with one optional field ŌĆö never a parallel factory.
Pattern.Why: One `OrderLifecycleContext` type, one `createOrderLifecycleContext` factory; the failure case overrides exactly one field and reuses everything else (AX_ONE_UNIFIED_CONTEXT_PER_FILE, AX_PREFER_FACTORY_OVER_HOOKS). No describe-scope `let`s; stubs flow through injection seams; no module-level mocks.

Pattern.Intent: Justified hook usage: SUT requires teardown-bound setup (fake timers + env stub). A SINGLE lifecycle context object is built in `beforeEach` and disposed in `afterEach`; tests consume fields off that context. No `let`-pileup.
Pattern.Why: Fake timers and env stubs are teardown-bound ŌĆö they cannot be cleaned by a factory return value (AX_HOOKS_ONLY_FOR_LIFECYCLE, AX_TIME_ENV_GLOBAL_LIFECYCLE). The describe holds exactly ONE `let ctx`, not a pile of `let sut, let tick, let env` (AX_NO_DESCRIBE_LET_PILEUP). The case reads `ctx.sut` / `ctx.tick` directly.

Pattern.Intent: Preferred posture: a SUT designed with injection seams is exercised against real `vi.fn()` stubs. No `vi.mock`, no operator confirm-gate triggered, no global patching ŌĆö yet the test is fully isolated from the network.
Pattern.Why: No `vi.mock`, no `vi.spyOn`, no `globalThis.fetch` patching: the SUT exposes injection seams (`httpRequest`, `store`) and the test passes typed `vi.fn()`s through them (AX_MOCK_AS_LAST_RESORT, AX_HTTP_BOUNDARY_INJECTION). The unified factory is reused; the case differs only by the `response` override.

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: - Pile of `let`-variables in describe scope (AX_NO_DESCRIBE_LET_PILEUP). - Per-case data built in `beforeEach` without any teardown reason (AX_HOOKS_ONLY_FOR_LIFECYCLE, AX_PREFER_FACTORY_OVER_HOOKS). - The second case re-assigns shared `sut`, leaking state across cases. - No unified context type/factory (AX_ONE_UNIFIED_CONTEXT_PER_FILE).

- Three parallel factories for one SUT in one file (violates AX_ONE_UNIFIED_CONTEXT_PER_FILE). - Each factory drifts independently; structural changes in `OrderLifecycle` require N edits, not one. - The reader-agent must learn three shapes instead of one + a set of overrides; file size grows non-linearly with case count.

- `vi.mock` introduced without operator confirm and without justification (AX_MOCK_AS_LAST_RESORT, AX_MOCK_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_CONFIRM). - `httpClient` is an in-process module ŌĆö the SUT could have accepted it as a constructor argument (AX_HTTP_BOUNDARY_INJECTION). - Module mocks freeze a snapshot of `httpClient`'s shape; real changes there will not be caught.

- Auto-update of stale snapshots with no operator visibility (AX_SNAPSHOT_OPERATOR_CONFIRM). - External snapshot of a small output that should be inline (AX_SNAPSHOT_USAGE_GATE). - Textbook fabrication path for green tests under agentic execution.

- Reliance on `globals: true` instead of explicit imports from `vitest` (AX_EXPLICIT_VITEST_IMPORTS). - Fake timers and env stubs enabled without paired teardown (AX_TIME_ENV_GLOBAL_LIFECYCLE). - State leaks into subsequent cases, producing order-dependent flicker.

- Manual `try/catch + expect.fail` instead of `rejects.toThrow` (AX_VITEST_ASSERT_API_CHOICE). - `toContain` on `error.message` (AX_PARTIAL_STRING_VIA_MATCH). - Inspecting `_inflightCount` ŌĆö protected internal field; `@ts-expect-error` bypasses encapsulation (inherited AX_CONTRACT_OVER_IMPLEMENTATION, AX_ISOLATION_WITH_BEHAVIORAL_EVIDENCE).

Workflow_Outline.Step: Read the contract and the ticket's `Test Scenario Coverage`. Derive happy-path, boundary, and failure scenarios. BDD scenarios in the ticket are the normative source of case names (see `Inherited_Baseline.BDD_Mapping_Ref`).

At the top of the test file define the SINGLE `XxxContext` type and the SINGLE factory `createXxxContext(overrides?)`. If teardown is required, define instead the SINGLE lifecycle context object built in `beforeEach`/`afterEach`. Choose ONE preparation path and stick to it for the whole file (AX_ONE_PREP_PATH_PER_DESCRIBE).

Estimate the file size for the cases planned. If the projection exceeds 300 LOC, plan a split now (by method or by scenario family) before writing the cases ŌĆö extracting later is more expensive (AX_TEST_FILE_SIZE_BUDGET).

For each `it`, decide trivial vs non-trivial. Non-trivial cases get the opening brief and phase anchors (inherited rules). Pass stubs through injection seams; do NOT introduce `vi.mock` without operator confirm.
Start with the simplest direct matcher. Aggregate into an OBSERVE phase only when a single rich diff beats several isolated assertions.

Re-check: unified context maintained, no parallel factories, file under budget, anchors paired, snapshots not auto-updated, no ungated mocks, diagnostic quality intact. Run type-check and tests with coverage. Declare any environment blocker EXPLICITLY.

Hook.Expected: Exit code 0; no `.only` / `.skip` / `.todo` without a deferred-ownership reference.
Exit code 0; the targeted file passes.
Exit code 0; Ōēź1 happy + Ōēź1 boundary (when applicable) + Ōēź1 failure-path per public method. Environment blockers declared EXPLICITLY.

Hook.Purpose: Flag test files that exceed the 500 LOC hard ceiling or the 300 LOC soft target (AX_TEST_FILE_SIZE_BUDGET).
Hook.Expected: No test file exceeds 500 code-LOC. Files over 300 carry a tracked split task or an explicit justification.

Hook.Purpose: Smoke-grep for forbidden patterns: `it.only`/`describe.only`/`it.skip`/`it.todo`, human narration `Step N`, `toContain` on `error.message`, manual `try/catch + expect.fail`.
Hook.Expected: Empty on new/changed test files (or matched lines carry an explicit deferred-ownership reference).

Hook.Purpose: Every `vi.mock(` / `vi.hoisted(` MUST be paired with a justification comment within 3 lines (manual review).
Hook.Expected: Each match has an immediate-neighbor comment naming the dependency and the reason injection is unavailable.

Hook.Purpose: Verify the agent did NOT run snapshot updates autonomously.
Hook.Expected: Snapshot diffs are presented to the operator BEFORE commit; no `vitest --update` / `vitest -u` invocation in the agent's command history.

Hook.Purpose: Smoke-grep for multiple `createXxxContext` definitions in the same file (AX_ONE_UNIFIED_CONTEXT_PER_FILE).
Hook.Command: rg --no-heading -nU "function create\w+Context" -t ts --glob '**/*.test.ts' | awk -F: '{c[$1]++} END {for (f in c) if (c[f] > 1) print c[f], f}'
Hook.Expected: No file declares more than one `createXxxContext` factory.

VitestRules.Reward_Criteria:
Ō£ģ REWARD: Exactly ONE `XxxContext` type and ONE `createXxxContext` factory per file; all cases consume it via overrides. New scenarios extend the existing factory rather than spawning parallel ones. Ō£ģ REWARD: Default preparation is the file's single factory called inside the case; describe-scope holds no mutable `let` populated by hooks. Ō£ģ REWARD: When hooks ARE used, they exist for genuine teardown reasons and expose a SINGLE lifecycle context object ŌĆö no `let`-pileup. Ō£ģ REWARD: Each describe uses ONE preparation path (factory-only OR lifecycle-context-only). Ō£ģ REWARD: Test files stay under 300 code-LOC (soft target) and never exceed 500 (hard ceiling); over-budget files are split by method or scenario family with the unified context preserved across the split. Ō£ģ REWARD: Real modules + dependency injection are the default; `vi.mock` / `vi.spyOn` / `vi.hoisted` absent unless paired with explicit justification AND operator confirmation. Ō£ģ REWARD: HTTP-touching code is tested via injected ports stubbed with `vi.fn()`; an existing project-level network-mocking harness (if any) is reused consistently. Ō£ģ REWARD: Fake timers, `vi.stubEnv`, `vi.stubGlobal` are paired with `vi.useRealTimers()` / `vi.unstubAll*()` (or runner config flags). Ō£ģ REWARD: Vitest APIs imported explicitly from `vitest`; default `isolate: true` preserved. Ō£ģ REWARD: Simplest direct matcher chosen (`toBe` / `toEqual` / `toStrictEqual` / `rejects.toThrow` / `toMatch`); error cases use `rejects.toThrow(SomeErrorClass)` + `toMatch` on the stable message part. Ō£ģ REWARD: Snapshots only for stable serializable outputs too large for inline literals; preferred form is `toMatchInlineSnapshot`; ALL updates pass the operator confirm-gate. Ō£ģ REWARD: Inherited rules (phase anchors, learning brief, BDD mapping, English-only, no human narration, contract coverage) honored.
ŌØī PENALTY: Multiple `createXxxContext` factories in one file, or ad-hoc inline construction alongside a factory. ŌØī PENALTY: `describe`-scope hosts mutable `let` variables populated in `beforeEach`. ŌØī PENALTY: `beforeEach` constructs values that have no teardown requirement. ŌØī PENALTY: Test file exceeds 500 code-LOC, or exceeds 300 without a tracked split task. ŌØī PENALTY: `vi.mock` / `vi.hoisted` without operator confirm-gate or without explicit justification. ŌØī PENALTY: Mocking the SUT's own module, the co-tested collaborator, or the very contract method under test. ŌØī PENALTY: Auto-updating snapshots (`vitest --update` / `vitest -u`) without operator review. ŌØī PENALTY: Snapshot used for a scalar or small object that would be clearer under `toBe` / `toEqual`. ŌØī PENALTY: Reliance on `globals: true` instead of explicit imports from `vitest`. ŌØī PENALTY: `toContain` on `error.message`, or manual `try/catch + expect.fail` instead of `rejects.toThrow`. ŌØī PENALTY: Fake timers / env stubs / global stubs enabled without paired teardown. ŌØī PENALTY: Test inspects protected/private/internal state of the SUT. ŌØī PENALTY: Phase anchors missing on a non-trivial case, OR phase anchors used around trivial one-line cases; anchor name restating syntax instead of intent. ŌØī PENALTY: Opening brief on a trivial case, OR missing brief on a non-trivial case. ŌØī PENALTY: A BDD scenario from the ticket has no corresponding `it` case OR has a mismatched canonical case name without updating the ticket. ŌØī PENALTY: Tests written for `runtime-hook-required` / `e2e-required` scenarios at the Vitest level without an explicit deferred-ownership note. ŌØī PENALTY: `console.*` in a test, `Step N` narration, duplicate cases, non-English names/comments. ŌØī PENALTY: Committed `it.only` / `describe.only` / `it.skip` / `it.todo` without deferred-ownership reference. ŌØī PENALTY: Type-check / coverage / test run failed silently without an EXPLICIT blocker statement.
